preliminary_imports.text: // #include "openssl/applink.c" // To prevent crashing (see the OpenSSL FAQ)
#include "openssl/ssl.h" // SSL and SSL_CTX for SSL connections

analysis_tasks.objective: Verify presence and initialization of OpenSSL in runtime initialization
analysis_tasks.rationale: SSL_library_init indicates explicit OpenSSL initialization; confirm location and usage.

analysis_tasks.objective: Locate and characterize SSL context creation points
analysis_tasks.scope: Call sites: SSL_CTX_new(...) variants in UtilitySSL.cpp
analysis_tasks.rationale: Creation of SSL_CTX determines server/client mode and method selection.
SSL_accept is the ingress TLS handshake; locate and extract surrounding logic.

analysis_tasks.scope: Call sites: SSL_connect() in SSLSocketInitiator.cpp and ThreadedSSLSocketInitiator.cpp
analysis_tasks.rationale: SSL_connect is the egress TLS handshake; extract call site and error handling.

analysis_tasks.scope: Call sites: SSL_set_bio(...) in multiple socket files
analysis_tasks.rationale: SSL_set_bio shows how sockets are wired to SSL objects; useful for I/O semantics.
Header imports are explicit evidence of OpenSSL usage at compile-time.
Dependency declaration shows OpenSSL is required when SSL is enabled.

analysis_tasks.objective: Collect certificate and private key artifacts present in repository
analysis_tasks.scope: Certificate and key files under bin/cfg/certs
analysis_tasks.rationale: Presence of PEM/CRT/KEY files indicates local certificate artifacts (likely test fixtures).
Scripts demonstrate how test certs are generated and may reveal key parameters or entropy sources used.

analysis_tasks.objective: Negative candidate: verify that FIX message headers with 'Sign' are protocol fields not cryptographic code
analysis_tasks.scope: Files matching 'Sign' in names under src/C++/fix*/
analysis_tasks.rationale: Lexical match 'Sign' appears in FIX message headers; need to confirm non-cryptographic use.

analysis_tasks.objective: Negative candidate: verify AssignmentReport/CollateralAssignment headers are protocol structures, not crypto
analysis_tasks.scope: Files: AssignmentReport.h and CollateralAssignment.h across FIX versions
analysis_tasks.rationale: Header names may trigger lexical crypto detectors; confirm not_crypto if no crypto operations present.

analysis_tasks.objective: Negative candidate: files with 'Key' in certs directory are artifacts, not application code
analysis_tasks.rationale: Ensure these are test keys/artifacts and not used as production secrets in code paths.

analysis_tasks.objective: Negative candidate: FIX field definitions for Signature/SecureData/EncryptMethod are protocol fields
analysis_tasks.rationale: Field classes named Signature/SecureData/EncryptMethod appear across language bindings and are protocol field definitions, not cryptographic implementations.

analysis_tasks.objective: Negative candidate: language bindings' field classes (Python/Ruby) for EncryptMethod/SecureData
analysis_tasks.scope: quickfix field class definitions in Python and Ruby
analysis_tasks.rationale: Generated binding files declare field types that mirror FIX spec fields; confirm no crypto usage.

analysis_tasks.objective: Negative candidate: FixFieldNumbers constants are numeric field identifiers, not crypto
analysis_tasks.rationale: Constants like Signature=89 are protocol identifiers; mark as not_crypto candidate.

evidence_records.explanation: Direct call to OpenSSL initialization functions in utility file.
Multiple SSL_CTX_new calls showing context creation variants.
Client-side TLS connect and handshake with error handling.
BIO binding demonstrating how socket I/O is connected to SSL object.

evidence_records.extracted_content:
#include "openssl/err.h"
#include "openssl/ssl.h" // SSL and SSL_CTX for SSL connections
evidence_records.explanation: Header includes confirm compile-time dependency on OpenSSL headers.
CMake declares OpenSSL as a required package when HAVE_SSL is enabled.

evidence_records.extracted_content: (PEM encoded private key contents omitted for brevity)
evidence_records.explanation: Private key artifact appears under bin/cfg and likely used as test fixture. Do not assume production use.

evidence_records.extracted_content:
openssl genrsa -des3 -out $base_name.key 2048
openssl rsa -in $base_name.key -out $base_name.key.insecure
openssl req -new -key $base_name.key -out $base_name.csr
openssl x509 -req -days 365 -in $base_name.csr -signkey $base_name.key -out $base_name.crt
evidence_records.explanation: Script uses OpenSSL CLI to generate keys and self-signed certs; shows test cert generation steps.

evidence_records.extracted_content:
#include "Message.h"
namespace FIX44 { class CollateralAssignment : public Message { ... FIELD_SET(*this, FIX::Signature); }
evidence_records.explanation: FIX message header fields include names like 'Signature' but are protocol field definitions, not cryptographic function calls; mark for human review as negative candidate.

evidence_records.extracted_content: (FIX message header structure declarations and FIELD_SET macros)
evidence_records.explanation: Protocol header structures; lexical match only.
Server key file present under bin/cfg; treat as test artifact unless used elsewhere.
Generated Python binding classes for FIX fields Signature/SecureData/EncryptMethod — protocol field definitions.

evidence_records.extracted_content: class Signature < Quickfix::StringField ... class SecureData < Quickfix::StringField ... class EncryptMethod < Quickfix::IntField
evidence_records.explanation: Ruby binding field class declarations mirror FIX spec fields; lexical match only.
Numeric field identifiers for protocol fields; not cryptographic implementations.
Executor config references server certificate and key files under cfg/certs; indicates runtime use of repo cert artifacts.
Tradeclient config contains SSLProtocol settings and commented client certificate entries; demonstrates runtime TLS config options and potential protocol selection.

context_bundles.reviewer_note: Check whether SSL_library_init is guarded by conditional build flags and whether alternative initialization paths exist.
Extract the exact method selection logic (TLS_server_method vs SSLv23) for protocol expectations.
Confirm how accept errors are handled and whether non-blocking behavior alters handshake semantics.
Check whether SNI is always set and how connection failures are reported.
Review the BIO flags and socket close semantics for potential resource or security implications.
Headers and autotools probes indicate compile-time OpenSSL expectations; check alternative include paths.
Verify required OpenSSL version and packaging used in CI.

known_limitations: Private key contents omitted from extracted_content to avoid exposing secrets in evidence export
context_bundles.reviewer_note: Confirm whether these keys are used only for tests and are not referenced by production configuration.
Script demonstrates test cert generation; reviewer should confirm whether parameters (key size, passphrase) are acceptable.
This is likely a not_crypto candidate; check for any function calls invoking cryptographic APIs within these headers (unlikely).
Protocol header; expected not_crypto outcome unless implementation code elsewhere performs crypto.

known_limitations: Key contents redacted in evidence export
context_bundles.reviewer_note: Confirm usage context of key files; if referenced by code/config, mark follow-up.
These are generated field definitions and numeric IDs from the FIX spec bindings; likely not_crypto.
Language binding files replicate protocol field definitions; check for any implementation-level crypto calls elsewhere.
Numeric constants mapping to field names; not cryptographic code.
Executor config references server certificate and key files under cfg/certs; indicates these artifacts are referenced by runtime configuration.
Tradeclient config exposes SSLProtocol options and commented client certificate file settings; useful to verify whether client certs are enabled in runtime.

human_analysis_records.summary: Repository explicitly initializes OpenSSL via SSL_library_init in UtilitySSL.cpp.
raw_observations: Observed SSL_library_init() and SSL_load_error_strings() in UtilitySSL.cpp
uncertainties: Cipher suites and protocol versions not observed in code
human_analysis_records.notes_for_planner: Context bundle sufficient to label as crypto_finding for OpenSSL init.
follow_up_suggestions: Collect runtime or build flags controlling OpenSSL initialization path.

human_analysis_records.summary: SSL_CTX contexts are created (server/client and SSLv23 variants) indicating protocol factory usage; SSLv23 use may indicate deprecated defaults.
raw_observations: Observed SSL_CTX_new(...) calls including SSLv23 methods
uncertainties: Exact negotiated TLS versions and cipher suites not present in code
human_analysis_records.notes_for_planner: Recommend follow-up to extract runtime config for cipher suites if deeper protocol assessment required.
follow_up_suggestions: Collect runtime or configuration files that specify cipher suites and protocol minimums.

reasoning_trace.interpretation: Possible use of deprecated protocol negotiation defaults

human_analysis_records.summary: Server-side TLS handshake invoked via SSL_accept in UtilitySSL.cpp.
raw_observations: Observed SSL_accept call site in UtilitySSL.cpp
uncertainties: Peer certificate validation behavior not fully shown in extracted range
human_analysis_records.notes_for_planner: Sufficient evidence for handshake call labeling; follow-up optional for validation policy details.
follow_up_suggestions: Collect runtime configuration or build flags that control OpenSSL initialization (e.g., checks for SSL_library_init guards or alternative init paths).

reasoning_trace.interpretation: OpenSSL is used for handshake implementation
Accept logic present in network path

human_analysis_records.summary: Client-side TLS handshake occurs via SSL_connect in SSLSocketInitiator and ThreadedSSLSocketInitiator.
raw_observations: Observed SSL_connect() with error handling in initiator files
uncertainties: Exact TLS parameters and verification chain not present
human_analysis_records.notes_for_planner: No further context required to label handshake usage.
follow_up_suggestions: Collect runtime configuration or code that specifies TLS methods and cipher suites (e.g., SSL_CTX_set_options, SSL_CTX_set_cipher_list).

human_analysis_records.summary: Socket BIOs are attached to SSL objects via SSL_set_bio, showing how sockets are wired into OpenSSL I/O.
uncertainties: Socket close semantics and BIO flags may affect resource handling
human_analysis_records.notes_for_planner: Sufficient context for BIO binding labeling.
follow_up_suggestions: Collect surrounding code that performs peer certificate validation and any configuration controlling verification behavior.

human_analysis_records.summary: OpenSSL headers are directly included in source headers, confirming compile-time OpenSSL usage.
raw_observations: Observed OpenSSL includes in headers and autotools probe
uncertainties: Which OpenSSL version is used at runtime not specified
human_analysis_records.notes_for_planner: Header includes are clear evidence of OpenSSL dependency.
follow_up_suggestions: Collect code ranges that set BIO flags or socket close semantics to confirm resource and security handling.

human_analysis_records.summary: CMake declares OpenSSL as a required dependency when HAVE_SSL is set.
uncertainties: Exact OpenSSL version not specified in CMake snippet
human_analysis_records.notes_for_planner: Dependency manifest confirms OpenSSL usage; consider collecting full build flags for version resolution.
follow_up_suggestions: Search for code paths or build flags that select specific SSL/TLS providers or versions (helpful to resolve deprecated API usage concerns).

reasoning_trace.interpretation: CMake requires OpenSSL when HAVE_SSL is used

uncertainties: Whether these keys are referenced by production configuration or are only test fixtures
